_comment: Single source of truth for CI-plane secret rotation (scripts/rotate_secret.py). Each entry is one rotatable secret and lists every place its value lives: GitHub Actions ENVIRONMENT secrets (repo + environment + secret_name) and/or non-Terraform Vercel project env vars (project_id + env_key). SCOPE: CI-plane credentials ONLY (build/deploy plumbing). App secrets in Terraform's SECRETS_INVENTORY (DB URLs, RSA keypair, KDF_SERVICE_KEY, STRIPE_*, OIDC client secrets, CRON_SECRET, etc.) are Terraform-owned — do NOT add them here; a direct write would drift. If one is ever added, set "terraform_managed": true and the engine refuses to write it and points to the terraform SECRETS_ROTATION runbook. 'kind' is the intended primary mode (paste|generate); 'generator' picks the generate strategy (vercel_token|random_urlsafe); 'per_env' makes generate mint a distinct value per environment; 'shared': true on a vercel_token entry mints ONE token (entry-level 'vercel_token_name') and writes the same value to every target (app/env filters ignored). Vercel entries whose project_id starts with 'TODO' are skipped with a warning.

secrets.name: GH_PACKAGES_PAT
secrets.description: Fine-grained PAT (contents:read on the private SDK repo) used at build time to pip-install kdf-auth-sdk. GitHub cannot mint PATs via API, so this is paste-only: owner creates the PAT, stages it in the SECRET_VALUE_NEW repo secret, then runs paste.

secrets.name: VERCEL_TOKEN
secrets.description: ONE shared Vercel deploy token used by every app's CI to deploy. Auto-minted via the Vercel API as a single token, written to every app repo's GitHub environment secret; the previous shared token is deleted only if ALL writes succeed (a partial failure keeps both valid so no repo holds a revoked token). App/env filters are ignored — it is one value everywhere. The terraform MANAGEMENT token is separate; see VERCEL_API_TOKEN.

secrets.name: VERCEL_API_TOKEN
secrets.description: Vercel token the terraform repo uses to MANAGE Vercel (create projects, set env vars) via the Terraform provider — kept separate from the shared deploy token (VERCEL_TOKEN) because it needs management scope, not just deploy. Auto-minted as one unique token, written to the terraform repo's infra environment secret, old token deleted. Rotate it on its own from the Ops form (generate, VERCEL_API_TOKEN) or via the monthly Rotate Vercel Tokens cron.

secrets.name: CICD_PAT
secrets.description: Fine-grained PAT (secrets:write + administration across the org repos). The rotation + kit workflows have moved to ephemeral GitHub App tokens (Phase 1, docs/design/github-app-migration.md), so CICD_PAT now survives ONLY as the fallback for those flows and as the live token for issue-create-repo.yml (creating a personal-account repo needs a user PAT — an App token can't, until the org move). GitHub has no PAT-creation API, so it is rotated BY HAND — monitored only (no engine targets). Recipe: docs/guides/SECRET_ROTATION.md §8.3.

secrets.name: CICD_REGISTRY_PAT
secrets.description: PAT for GitHub Packages / registry access. Rotated by hand (no PAT-creation API). Monitored only. Recipe: docs/guides/SECRET_ROTATION.md §8.5.

secrets.name: VERCEL_MASTER_TOKEN
secrets.description: Full-Account Vercel token (kdf-master-rotation) the engine uses to mint/delete per-app Vercel tokens. Rotated by hand today (recipe §8.4); monitored only. Auto-rotation is a planned enhancement (docs/design/github-app-migration.md) — deferred because the Vercel create-token API's scope preservation is unverified.

secrets.name: KDF_APP_PRIVATE_KEY
secrets.description: Private key (.pem) for the KDF GitHub App, stored as a kriegerdataforge-cicd repo secret (alongside the non-secret KDF_APP_ID). The rotation + kit workflows mint short-lived installation tokens from it (actions/create-github-app-token) instead of using long-lived PATs. It has no expiry, but rotate it periodically; GitHub Apps support multiple private keys for zero-downtime rotation. Monitored only (no engine targets). Recipe: docs/guides/SECRET_ROTATION.md §8.3a.
